audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Nazirite vow and priestly blessing text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for voluntary Nazirite vow by man or woman, grape-product abstention, uncut hair, corpse-contact restriction, accidental defilement, completion offerings, and Aaronic blessing.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When man or woman makes a special vow, vow of Nazirite, to separate/consecrate oneself to YHWH,
from wine and strong drink he shall separate; vinegar of wine and vinegar of strong drink he shall not drink, and any juice of grapes he shall not drink, and fresh grapes or dried he shall not eat.
All days of his separation/consecration, from anything made from grapevine, from seeds even to skin, he shall not eat.

All days of vow of his separation/consecration, razor shall not pass over his head; until days are fulfilled for which he separated himself to YHWH, holy he shall be, letting locks of hair of his head grow.
All days he separates himself to YHWH, to dead soul/life he shall not come.
For father or mother, brother or sister, he shall not make himself unclean by them when they die, for consecration of his Divine is upon his head.
All days of his separation/consecration he is holy to YHWH.
If one dies suddenly beside him and he makes head of his consecration unclean, he shall shave his head on day of his cleansing; on seventh day he shall shave it.
On eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon to priest, to entrance of tent of meeting.
Priest shall make one as sin/purification offering and one as burnt offering, and make atonement/covering for him because he sinned/misaligned by dead soul/life; he shall sanctify his head that day.
He shall separate to YHWH days of his consecration and bring male lamb, son of a year, as guilt/reparation offering; former days shall fall away because his consecration became unclean.
This is Torah/instruction of Nazirite: on day days of his consecration are fulfilled, he shall be brought to entrance of tent of meeting.

He shall bring near his offering to YHWH: one complete male lamb, son of a year, as burnt offering; one complete female lamb, daughter of a year, as sin/purification offering; and one complete ram as peace/well-being offering,
and basket of unleavened breads of fine flour, cakes mixed with oil and wafers of unleavened bread anointed with oil, with their grain offering and drink offerings.
Priest shall bring them near before YHWH and make his sin/purification offering and burnt offering.
The ram he shall make as sacrifice of peace/well-being to YHWH with basket of unleavened bread; priest shall make its grain offering and drink offering.
Nazirite shall shave head of his consecration at entrance of tent of meeting, take hair of head of his consecration, and put it on fire under sacrifice of peace/well-being.
Priest shall take boiled shoulder from ram, one unleavened cake from basket, and one unleavened wafer, and put them on palms of Nazirite after he has shaved his consecration.
Priest shall wave them as wave offering before YHWH; holy it is for priest, with breast of wave offering and thigh of contribution; afterward Nazirite may drink wine.

This is Torah/instruction of Nazirite who vows his offering to YHWH according to his separation/consecration, besides what his hand reaches; according to vow he vows, so he shall do, according to Torah/instruction of his consecration.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: Thus you shall bless children of Israel, saying to them:
May YHWH bless you and keep you.
May YHWH make his face shine upon you and be gracious to you.
May YHWH lift up his face toward you and give you peace/shalom.
They shall put my name upon children of Israel, and I myself will bless them.

translation_notes: The Nazirite vow is available to man or woman, an important shared-access feature.
Nazir terminology includes separation/consecration; avoid equating holiness with disgust toward ordinary food, drink, hair, or family grief.
Accidental corpse defilement resets the vow but should not be framed as moral blame for an unexpected death.
Completion includes burnt, purification, peace/well-being, grain, and drink offerings, followed by renewed permission to drink wine.
The Aaronic blessing culminates in shalom: wholeness, peace, well-being, and restored relational harmony.
The priests place YHWH's name on Israel, but the text says YHWH is the one who blesses.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 6 to pressure people into ascetic vows, shame alcohol abstainers or drinkers, condemn hair choices, suppress grief, or blame someone for accidental ritual interruption. The vow is voluntary and time-bounded; the chapter culminates in grace and shalom.
